seo.description: Unity Scanner — unified Editor and MCP tool for project health analysis across dependencies, materials, shaders, Addressables, and more.

props.title: Unity Scanner
paragraphs: Unified Unity Editor and MCP tool for project health — one window for dependency scans, material and shader audits, Addressables layout checks, and automated CI gates.
Run from Tools > Unity Scanner, batch mode, or AI assistants via the MCP server.

apps.title: Unity Scanner
apps.text_1: Unified Editor and MCP scanner for Unity project health.
apps.text_2: Analyze dependencies, shaders, Addressables, and many other categories from one tool.

props.title: Batch API
props.intro: Programmatic entry points for CI and batch mode. All runners return BatchResult and accept an optional BatchOptions.

entries.label: RunAll
entries.description: Runs all enabled analysis categories sequentially and aggregates results.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration (categories, platform profile, fail severity, output path).

entries.label: RunDependencies
entries.description: Scans the dependencies category — finds unreferenced and unused assets via reverse-dependency map.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration.

entries.label: RunMissingReferences
entries.description: Scans scenes, prefabs, and ScriptableObjects for broken GUID/FileID references and missing scripts.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration.

entries.label: RunMaterials
entries.description: Scans the materials category — null/builtin materials, missing shaders, duplicates, and SRP batcher compatibility.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration.

entries.label: RunTextures
entries.description: Scans SpriteAtlases and Texture2D assets for atlas duplicates, compression issues, and oversized textures.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration.

entries.label: RunAddressables
entries.description: Parses Addressables Build Layout for circular dependencies, remote dependency issues, and duplicate assets.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Set BuildLayoutPath for the layout file to analyze.

entries.label: RunShaderAnalysis
entries.description: Finds error shaders, variant explosion, expensive keywords, and render pipeline mismatches.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration; PlatformProfile adjusts variant thresholds.

entries.label: RunTerrainAnalysis
entries.description: Checks terrain colliders, layers, control map memory, and density overages.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration.

entries.label: RunFontTextAnalysis
entries.description: Detects TMP atlas growth risks, oversized atlases, and deep fallback chains.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration.

entries.label: RunAudioAnalysis
entries.description: Finds load-type mismatches, oversized clips, and duplicate audio files.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration.

entries.label: RunAnimationAnalysis
entries.description: Detects unreachable states, missing clips, state machine complexity, and duplicate clips.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration.

entries.label: RunScenePrefabHealth
entries.description: Checks deep nesting, override explosion, broken references, and inactive object anti-patterns.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration.

entries.label: RunBuildPlatformReadiness
entries.description: Validates import policies, platform compatibility, stripping risks, and startup budgets.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: PlatformProfile and FailOnSeverity are commonly set for CI gates.

entries.label: RunParticleAnalysis
entries.description: Audits particle systems for emission limits, module count, trails, collision, and overdraw risks.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration with particle-specific thresholds.

entries.label: RunUICanvasAnalysis
entries.description: Checks UI canvases for raycast targets, TMP mixing, vertex count, and nesting depth.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration with UI-specific thresholds.

entries.label: RunLightingAnalysis
entries.description: Audits lighting for emissive GI, mixed-mode consistency, realtime light counts, and probe usage.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration with lighting-specific thresholds.

entries.label: RunLODAnalysis
entries.description: Checks LOD groups for missing levels, material mismatches, and screen transition settings.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration with LOD-specific thresholds.

entries.label: RunPhysicsAnalysis
entries.description: Audits rigidbody counts, mesh collider complexity, layer matrix, and physics materials.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration with physics-specific thresholds.

entries.label: RunAsmDefAudit
entries.description: Audits assembly definitions for circular references, editor leakage, platform filters, and duplicates.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration with asmdef audit flags.

entries.label: RunProjectHealth
entries.description: Detects empty folders, orphaned .meta files, broken assets, empty scenes, and oversized directories.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration.

entries.label: RunSprite2DAnalysis
entries.description: Checks 2D sprites for atlas unused ratio, packing issues, duplicates, and polygon vertex counts.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration with sprite-specific thresholds.

entries.label: RunRegressionTrend
entries.description: Compares scan results against a saved baseline to detect regressions and improvements.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Set BaselinePath and RegressionThreshold for CI regression gates.

entries.label: RunSelectedCategories
entries.signature: BatchResult RunSelectedCategories(string[] categoryIds, BatchOptions options = null)
entries.description: Runs a custom list of categories by ID in order.

arguments.name: categoryIds
arguments.description: Category IDs to scan (e.g. dependencies, materials, project_health).

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Optional batch configuration.

props.title: MCP Architecture
paragraphs: The MCP server uses a bridge architecture. Each tool call launches Unity in batch mode; results are written to a temp file and returned as text and JSON.
props.pre: AI Client → stdio JSON-RPC → Node.js MCP Server → Unity batch mode → UnityScanner API → JSON result file

props.title: MCP Server
props.intro: Tools exposed to AI coding assistants. Each call runs a fresh Unity batch instance (~10–30s startup).

entries.label: unity_scanner_run_all
entries.description: Run all enabled UnityScanner categories and return a full project health report. This is the most comprehensive scan.
entries.example: "Run a full project scan and tell me what's wrong"

entries.label: unity_scanner_run_category
entries.description: Run a specific UnityScanner category by ID. Use unity_scanner_list_categories to discover available IDs.

arguments.name: category
arguments.description: Category ID to scan (e.g. dependencies, shader_analysis, project_health).

entries.label: unity_scanner_run_selected
entries.description: Run multiple selected UnityScanner categories by their IDs.

arguments.name: categories
arguments.description: Array of category IDs to scan (e.g. ["dependencies", "materials", "project_health"]).

entries.label: unity_scanner_list_categories
entries.description: List all available UnityScanner categories with their IDs and enabled status.

entries.label: unity_scanner_get_settings
entries.description: Get current UnityScanner settings including active platform profile and category enable/disable states.

entries.label: unity_scanner_set_profile
entries.description: Set the platform profile for threshold comparisons. Profiles adjust severity thresholds for mobile, console, or desktop targets.

arguments.name: profile
arguments.description: Platform profile: mobile, console, or desktop.

entries.example: "Set the platform profile to mobile, then run project_health and build_platform_readiness"

entries.label: unity_scanner_regression_check
entries.description: Compare current scan results against a previously saved baseline to detect regressions (new issues).

arguments.name: baseline_path
arguments.description: Path to the baseline JSON file to compare against.

entries.label: unity_scanner_baseline_create
entries.description: Run a full scan and save results as a baseline snapshot for future regression comparisons.
entries.example: "Create a baseline snapshot of current project health"
